2604.21525.title: Job Skill Extraction via LLM-Centric Multi-Module Framework
2604.21525.paper_summary: Span-level skill extraction from job advertisements underpins candidate-job matching and labor-market analytics, yet generative large language models (LLMs) often yield malformed spans, boundary drift, and hallucinations, especially with long-tail terms and cross-domain shift. We present SRICL, an LLM-centric framework that combines semantic retrieval (SR), in-context learning (ICL), and supervised fine-tuning (SFT) with a deterministic verifier. SR pulls in-domain annotated sentences and definitions from ESCO to form format-constrained prompts that stabilize boundaries and handle coordination. SFT aligns output behavior, while the verifier enforces pairing, non-overlap, and BIO legality with minimal retries. On six public span-labeled corpora of job-ad sentences across sectors and languages, SRICL achieves substantial STRICT-F1 improvements over GPT-3.5 prompting baselines and sharply reduces invalid tags and hallucinated spans, enabling dependable sentence-level deployment in low-resource, multi-domain settings.
2604.21525.paper_summary_zh: 從職位廣告中提取技能的跨度是候選人與職位匹配以及勞動市場分析的基礎，但生成式大型語言模型（LLMs）經常產生不正確的跨度、邊界漂移和幻覺，特別是在長尾術語和跨領域轉移的情況下。我們提出了SRICL，這是一個以LLM為中心的框架，結合了語義檢索（SR）、上下文學習（ICL）和有監督的微調（SFT），以及一個確定性驗證器。SR從ESCO中提取領域內的標註句子和定義，以形成格式受限的提示，穩定邊界並處理協調。SFT對齊輸出行為，而驗證器則以最小的重試強制配對、非重疊和BIO合法性。在六個涵蓋各行業和語言的公共跨度標註語料庫上，SRICL在GPT-3.5提示基準上實現了顯著的STRICT-F1改進，並大幅減少了無效標籤和幻覺跨度，使得在低資源、多領域環境中可靠的句子級部署成為可能。
2604.21525.author: Guojing Li et.al.
2604.21525.authors: Guojing Li, Zichuan Fu, Junyi Li, Faxue Liu, Wenxia Zhou, Yejing Wang, Jingtong Gao, Maolin Wang, Rungen Liu, Wenlin Zhang, Xiangyu Zhao
